title: Bahamas Sea Level Sensor Network
text:
The Bahamas Sea Level Sensor Project aims to provide water level data throughout The Bahamas.
This website is a work in progress and will continuously be updated throughout Spring and Summer 2026. Stay tuned for more information.

title: Bahamas Water Level Sensor Project
text:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load the data
# skiprows=600 ignores the first 600 lines
# sep='\t' assumes a tab separator; change to ',' if it's a true CSV
df = pd.read_csv(
    'data-mar12.csv', 
    skiprows=600, 
    sep=',', 
    header=None, 
    names=['timestamp', 'value']
)

# Convert the timestamp column to actual datetime objects
df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'])

print(df.head())

            timestamp   value
0 2026-03-10 20:59:47  1735.0
1 2026-03-10 20:59:48  1730.0
2 2026-03-10 20:59:49  1740.0
3 2026-03-10 20:59:50  5000.0
4 2026-03-10 20:59:51  5000.0



# 3. Create the plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.scatter(df['timestamp'], df['value'])

# 4. Formatting the chart
plt.title('Value vs Time')
plt.xlabel('Timestamp')
plt.ylabel('Value (mm)')
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)

# Rotate date labels for better readability
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()

# 5. Display the plot
plt.show()









df2 = pd.read_csv(
    'data-mar16.csv', 
    skiprows=1, 
    sep=',', 
    header=None, 
    names=['timestamp', 'P1', 'P2', 'P3', 'P4', 'P5', 'P6', 'P7', 'P8', 'P9', 'P10', 'P11', 'P12', 'P13' ]

for col in df2.columns[1:]:
    plt.scatter(df2["timestamp"], df2[col], marker='o', label=col)

plt.xlabel("Time")
plt.ylabel("Pressure Value")
plt.title("Sensor Pressure Readings")
plt.legend(ncol=3)
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()

title: Sensor Network
text:
library(leaflet)

# Define data
sensors <- data.frame(
  name = c("Nassau", "Freeport", "Exuma"),
  lat = c(25.0443, 26.5285, 23.6190),
  lon = c(-77.3504, -78.6954, -75.9695)
)

# Build map (NO pipes to avoid dependency issues)
m <- leaflet()

m <- addProviderTiles(m, providers$CartoDB.Positron)
m <- setView(m, lng = -76.5, lat = 24.5, zoom = 6)

m <- addMarkers(
  m,
  lng = sensors$lon,
  lat = sensors$lat,
  popup = sensors$name,
  label = sensors$name
)

m

title: Sensor Network
text:
library(leaflet)

# Define data
sensors <- data.frame(
  name = c("Nassau", "Freeport", "Exuma"),
  lat = c(25.0443, 26.5285, 23.6190),
  lon = c(-77.3504, -78.6954, -75.9695)
)

# Build map (NO pipes to avoid dependency issues)
m <- leaflet()

m <- addProviderTiles(m, providers$CartoDB.Positron)
m <- setView(m, lng = -76.5, lat = 24.5, zoom = 6)

m <- addMarkers(
  m,
  lng = sensors$lon,
  lat = sensors$lat,
  popup = sensors$name,
  label = sensors$name
)

m

title: About
text:
Add information about the sensors here. There should be info about the actual hardward, the data collection and metadata, the software used, the cloud.
There should also be information about the team and the project? But maybe that can go on the home page.
